titleStmt.title: Letter from M. Mattei to Colonel O'Grady, 5 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: M. Mattei

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter form M. Mattei to the Chief Secretary's Office in Dublin Castle. The letter is written during the period of martial law in Ireland following the Easter Rising which resulted in strict regulations on movement in and out of Ireland. The letter requests a permit for Mattein to go to Ireland and visit her mother in Law.

msIdentifier.collection: National Archive of Ireland-Chief Secretary's Office-Registered Papers, NAI/CSO/RP/1916/5611/8242/3

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Montague House 3 Lansdowne Rd. E. Croydon May 5th 1916 Dear Sir I want to go to
Ireland, and I
find I must get a
permit. Will you
please let me know
where I have to
apply for one.
The place I want
to go is my mother in laws, Colonel O'Grady's Kilballyowen Kilmallock Co Limerick Yrs truly
(Mrs.) M Mattei
